question:
Bitcoin new all time high by Friday?

Description: On March 5, Bitcoin hit an all time high, with the highest candlestick on Binance BTC_USDT displaying a price of 69,000.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if Bitcoin (BTC) reaches a price greater than 69,000.00 for the first time according to Binance's candlestick prices by March 8, 2024, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market will be the 30-min candlestick high prices from Binance (https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT). Note: The final 30 minute candle from 11:30 to 12:00 counts. If Binance is unavailable, a consensus of other credible sources will be used.

evidences.title: Bitcoin Surges: Dramatic 1.97% Five-Minute Rally on Binance USDT Market
evidences.content: ### The Role of Market Liquidity and Structure Market structure plays a crucial role in facilitating rapid price changes. The BTC/USDT pair on Binance typically boasts some of the deepest liquidity in the cryptocurrency ecosystem. However, even deep markets can experience temporary imbalances. Order book analysis often reveals that during such spikes, the density of sell orders at certain price levels diminishes rapidly. This phenomenon allows aggressive buying pressure to push the price upward with less resistance, a scenario frequently documented in market microstructure research. ## Contextualizing Cryptocurrency Market Volatility [...] ## Conclusion The dramatic 1.97% Bitcoin price surge within five minutes on Binance serves as a potent case study in cryptocurrency market dynamics. It underscores the interplay of liquidity, algorithmic trading, derivatives, and global sentiment that defines modern digital asset markets. While such volatility presents trading opportunities, it also reinforces the necessity for disciplined risk management and a focus on long-term fundamentals. As the asset class continues to mature, understanding these rapid movements remains essential for all...

evidences.title: Binance Historical Tick Data (1 second) for a specific date range and symbol - Stack Overflow
evidences.content: ``` 1688169600000,30471.99000000,30472.00000000,30471.99000000,30472.00000000,0.54114000,1688169600999,16489.61790060,31,0.52320000,15942.95040000,0 ``` enables you to calculate this: From millisecond timestamp `1688169600000` (July 1st, 2023 0:00:00 UTC) To millisecond timestamp `1688169600999` (July 1st, 2023 0:00:00.999 UTC) Full candle + Open price `30471.99` USDT for 1 BTC + Close price `30472.00` USDT for 1 BTC + Close is higher than open => candle is green Knots + Lowest price `30471.99` USDT for 1 BTC - Same as open price of green candle => no knot below + Highest price `30472.00` USDT for 1 BTC - Same as close price of green candle => no knot above Share Improve this answer answered Aug 21, 2023 at 21:45 Petr Hejda's user avatar Petr Hejda [...] ``` #!/bin/bash for i in $(seq -f "%02g" 1 31) # zero-padded days 01, 02, 03, ... 31 do # month of july 2023, day number is the `i` variable archiveName="BTCUSDT-1s-2023-07-${i}.zip" # download the .zip, extract it (saves .csv), and remove the .zip wget " && unzip "./${archiveName}" && rm "./${archiveName}" done ``` As mentioned in their GitHub readme, each of the the `.csv` lines contains the following field values: | Open time...

evidences.content: %('!#$ % 𝑞!$% # Figure 17 - Average hourly spread on BTC/USDT trading on Binance. The end of 0-fee trading is easily recognizable Source: Crypto Lake API, original computation and visualization via Python Computed 𝑒s will then be summed together over different periods of time (i.e., upsampled) to form OFIs. Figure 18 shows the average hourly 𝑒 for the year in consideration. A change in the value of 𝑒 is evident after commission-free trading is removed. The sharp increase in volatility is most likely due to the thinner liquidity caused by users leaving the exchange as a result of the new fee structure, effectively causing each order book event to have a higher impact on the order book state compared to the period when commission-free trading attracted enough traders to have more resistant [...] the two: 𝑚𝑖𝑑_𝑝𝑟𝑖𝑐𝑒= 𝑎𝑠𝑘_0_𝑝𝑟𝑖𝑐𝑒 + 𝑏𝑖𝑑_0_𝑝𝑟𝑖𝑐𝑒 2 Variables are named as they are in the code created to download, manipulate and visualize this data, available in the Appendix. Converting Timestamps to Unix Timestamps To retrieve data from the API, we’ll need to send Unix timestamps as parameters. Here’s an example of how to convert our datetime objects to Unix timestamps in milliseconds: # Convert the times to Unix timestamps in milliseconds start_timestamp = int(start_time.timestamp() 1000) end_timestamp = int(end_time.timestamp() 1000) ### 4. evidences.title: Bitcoin (BTC) Price Prediction 2023, 2024, 2025-2030
evidences.content: At the time of analysis, the price of Bitcoin (BTC) was recorded at $0.6651. evidences.query: site:developers.binance.com kline endpoint BTCUSDT 30m high documentation interval limit earliest timestamp

evidences.title: Market - Spot REST API | Binance Developer Docs
evidences.content: If timeZone provided, kline intervals are interpreted in that timezone instead of UTC. Note that startTime and endTime are always interpreted in UTC
evidences.query: site:developers.binance.com kline endpoint BTCUSDT 30m high documentation interval limit earliest timestamp

evidences.title: Kline Candlestick Streams | Binance Open Platform
evidences.content: ## Update Speed​ 250ms ## Response Example​ `{ "e": "kline", // Event type "E": 1638747660000, // Event time "s": "BTCUSDT", // Symbol "k": { "t": 1638747660000, // Kline start time "T": 1638747719999, // Kline close time "s": "BTCUSDT", // Symbol "i": "1m", // Interval "f": 100, // First trade ID "L": 200, // Last trade ID "o": "0.0010", // Open price "c": "0.0020", // Close price "h": "0.0025", // High price "l": "0.0015", // Low price "v": "1000", // Base asset volume "n": 100, // Number of trades "x": false, // Is this kline closed? "q": "1.0000", // Quote asset volume "V": "500", // Taker buy base asset volume "Q": "0.500", // Taker buy quote asset volume "B": "123456" // Ignore } }` [...] Binance Logo Binance Logo # Kline/Candlestick Streams ## Stream Description​ The Kline/Candlestick Stream push updates to the current klines/candlestick every 250 milliseconds (if existing). Kline/Candlestick chart intervals: m -> minutes; h -> hours; d -> days; w -> weeks; M -> months ## URL PATH​ `/market` `/market` ## Stream Name​ `<symbol>@kline_<interval>` `<symbol>@kline_<interval>` ## Update Speed​ 250ms ## Response Example​ [...] After CM migration, both `fstream` and `dstream` ma...
evidences.query: site:developers.binance.com kline endpoint BTCUSDT 30m high documentation interval limit earliest timestamp

evidences.title: Market Data - Options REST API | Binance Developer Docs
evidences.content: Kline/Candlestick Data › Query Parameters ... If startTime and endTime are not sent, the most recent klines are returned. Kline/Candlestick Data › Responses.
